description: Evidence-informed coping skills organized by target emotion

strategies.name: 5-4-3-2-1 grounding
strategies.instructions: Name 5 things you can see. 4 things you can touch. 3 things you can hear. 2 things you can smell. 1 thing you can taste. Say them out loud or in your head.
contraindications: none significant

strategies.name: box breathing
strategies.instructions: Inhale for 4 counts. Hold for 4 counts. Exhale for 4 counts. Hold for 4 counts. Repeat 4-8 times.
contraindications: may increase anxiety in some with trauma history

strategies.name: progressive muscle relaxation
strategies.instructions: Tense each muscle group for 5 seconds, then release. Work from toes to head. Notice the contrast between tension and relaxation.
contraindications: muscle injuries
chronic pain conditions without adaptation

strategies.name: thought record
strategies.instructions: Write down the situation, automatic thought, emotion, evidence for and against the thought, and a balanced alternative thought.
contraindications: may feel overwhelming in acute crisis

strategies.name: behavioral activation scheduling
strategies.instructions: Schedule small activities that provide accomplishment or pleasure. Start very small (5-10 min). Rate expected vs actual mood change. Gradually increase.
contraindications: immediate crisis
severe fatigue from medical causes

strategies.name: self-compassion break
strategies.instructions: Acknowledge suffering ('this is hard'), recognize common humanity ('I'm not alone in this'), offer self-kindness ('may I be kind to myself').
contraindications: may feel uncomfortable initially for those with deep shame

strategies.name: worry time scheduling
strategies.instructions: Set aside 15 minutes at the same time daily to worry. At other times, postpone worries to worry time. During worry time, write out all concerns.
contraindications: may not work for generalized anxiety without professional guidance

strategies.name: gratitude journaling
strategies.instructions: Write 3 things you're grateful for daily. Be specific. Can include small things (a warm cup of tea). Focus on different things each day.
contraindications: may feel forced in severe depression

strategies.name: safe place visualization
strategies.instructions: Close your eyes. Imagine a place where you feel completely safe. Engage all senses — what do you see, hear, smell, feel? Stay here for several breaths.
contraindications: may be triggering for some trauma survivors
dissociation risk

strategies.name: TIPP skills (DBT)
strategies.instructions: Temperature (cold water on face), Intense exercise (jumping jacks), Paced breathing, Progressive muscle relaxation. Use during emotional crisis.
contraindications: cold water not safe for some heart conditions

strategies.name: cognitive defusion
strategies.instructions: Notice the thought ('I notice I'm having the thought that I'm a failure'). Add 'I notice' prefix. Say it in a silly voice. Imagine it on a leaf floating by.
contraindications: may feel dismissive if not introduced properly

strategies.name: opposite action
strategies.instructions: Identify the urge (withdraw, avoid, attack). Do the opposite (reach out, approach, gentle disengage). Notice how the emotion shifts.
best_situations: when acting on the urge would make things worse
contraindications: not appropriate when safety is at risk

strategies.name: mindful walking
strategies.instructions: Walk slowly. Notice each step. Feel the ground. Notice your surroundings with all senses. Return attention to walking when mind wanders.
contraindications: mobility limitations
unsafe walking environment

strategies.name: values-based action
strategies.instructions: Identify what matters to you. Choose one small action aligned with that value. Do it with attention and intention. Repeat daily.
contraindications: not suitable during acute crisis

strategies.name: sleep hygiene protocol
strategies.instructions: Consistent wake time. No screens 1 hour before bed. Cool dark room. No caffeine after 2pm. Wind-down routine. Get up if can't sleep for 20 min.
contraindications: may need professional help for severe insomnia

strategies.name: anger journaling
strategies.instructions: Write out what happened, what you felt, the intensity (1-10), the trigger, and what you needed. Do not censor. Write and then review later if helpful.
contraindications: rumination risk if not structured

strategies.name: social connection scheduling
strategies.instructions: Schedule one low-pressure social interaction per week. Could be coffee with a friend, joining a group, or a phone call. Start with safe, comfortable people.
contraindications: social anxiety may need graded approach

strategies.name: grounding through senses
strategies.instructions: Hold an ice cube. Smell a strong scent (peppermint, citrus). Touch different textures. Eat something with strong flavor. Use strong sensations to return to present.
contraindications: avoid pain-based grounding for self-harm urges

strategies.name: thought stopping technique
strategies.instructions: Notice the rumination. Say (out loud or in head) 'STOP.' Redirect to a pre-chosen neutral or positive thought. Practice repeatedly.
contraindications: may not work for OCD without professional guidance
suppression may worsen for some
